item.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum controversy at Great Wall yoga event
item.description: Canadian athleisure company says it ‘should have been more cautious and thorough’

item.title: Is Lloyd’s of London the world’s oldest podshop?
item.description: No. But it’s sort of a bit like one

item.title: FirstFT: Investors pile into dollar
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Bezos buys into UK AI start-up and former executive sues Citi

item.title: Tech Exchange
item.description: This series features conversations between FT journalists and the technology industry’s leaders, innovators and thinkers. In the latest instalment, Luciana Lixandru, global co-lead of Sequoia’s early-stage investment business, talks about why it is time for ‘act two’ for Europe’s tech sector

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: Elon Musk: Wall Street’s new roll-up artist
item.description: SpaceX’s IPO has given the trillionaire even more firepower for acquisitions

item.title: Amazon backs AI start-up developing models to simulate physical world
item.description: Company joins investment arms of Nvidia and AMD in $310mn funding round for Odyssey ML

item.title: Jeff Bezos backs UK start-up CuspAI in $400mn funding round
item.description: New financing will more than quadruple the two-year-old AI company’s valuation to $2.6bn

item.title: Defence not likely to be a panacea for European carmakers
item.description: An expansion into a surging industry might create buzz but it is not likely to have a big impact

item.title: The oil shortage is ending — and now comes the glut
item.description: Once flows resume properly, there will be a lot of extra oil in the system

item.title: VW resorts to sealed bids to avoid conflicts in $10bn engines sale
item.description: German carmaker moves to counter fears EQT has edge after teaming up with major shareholders

item.title: AI and tech groups not in ‘a bubble situation’, says Schroders CIO
item.description: UK asset manager is optimistic in year of historically large tech IPOs

item.title: How will a judge value my business?
item.description: I’m getting divorced and I’m really concerned about my finances

item.title: The long way back from the Iran energy shock
item.description: Oil production facilities and markets will take months or years to work through consequences of Strait of Hormuz closure

item.title: Wartime jolt pushes buttons on renewable power
item.description: Response to Middle East energy crisis varies widely across the globe

item.title: We have to manage the AI revolution
item.description: There must be a global agreement on how the technology is controlled

item.title: China’s tech champion tests limits of US chip controls
item.description: Seven years after being written off the Chinese tech giant is making technical advances that appear to sidestep Washington curbs

item.title: Co-owning a holiday home has a luxe new look
item.description: Despite continued nervousness about sharing a property, those who love it are all in — and demanding upscale new options

item.title: Sequoia’s Luciana Lixandru: Can Europe build a trillion-dollar tech company?
item.description: The global co-lead of the US venture capital firm’s early-stage investment business talks about why it is time for ‘act two’ for Europe’s tech sector

item.title: Data centre deals attract billions despite geopolitical tensions
item.description: Sector remains hot despite some investors’ wariness about tenants such as ByteDance

item.title: Now is not the time to weaken the UK’s bank rules
item.description: We should be clear about who is likely to benefit from an easing in the leverage ratio

item.title: The financial scams that brought Albania to the brink of war
item.description: 1997 was the country’s year of financial horror

item.title: Ambani set to fire starting gun on India’s largest IPO
item.description: Iran war has hit country’s equity market and disrupted what was positioned to be another record year for flotations

item.title: Snap defies investor pressure with ‘Specs’ smart glasses launch
item.description: Chief Evan Spiegel describes $2,195 eyewear as his ‘life’s work’ as he faces increasing criticism over costs of project

item.title: SpaceX leapfrogs Amazon to become world’s fifth-most valuable company
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI, rocket and satellite group also announces $60bn deal for AI coding app Cursor

item.title: Ex-Citi executive says bank sacked her after raising Trump concerns
item.description: Former employee claims in lawsuit that she identified issues with risk-management practices

item.title: BMW cuts profit guidance as Iran war adds to challenges in Chinese market
item.description: Carmaker expects operating margin in its automotive division to be 1-3% in 2026, a sharp fall from previous projections

item.title: The White House’s capricious controls on Anthropic
item.description: An opaque approach to US policy risks hampering AI development

item.title: Australia-style approaches to social media ban may not work, Ofcom warns
item.description: Government considers putting age limits on virtual private networks to help enforce social media restrictions

item.title: GM and Lockheed Martin partner on boosting weapons production
item.description: Pentagon requested tie-up to help defence group boost its output of critical munitions
